text.title: Continuous Discovery Agent Pipeline
text.summary: Planning-lens pipeline bridging research collection to delivery, subagent table, prompt pattern, and critical gotchas for LLM-driven OST management.

section.title: Pipeline Position
section.p: This methodology is the product-planning lens — downstream of research collection (pro/research/researcher/continuous-discovery/) and upstream of delivery (SDD). Raw signals reach the OST curator; the curator feeds the opportunity-scorer; scored opportunities feed the trio-deliberation agent which produces specs with opp_id references consumed by faion-sdd-executor-agent.

section.title: Subagent Roles and Models
section.p: intake-classifier (haiku, real-time): classifies requests as opportunity | solution | bug. ost-curator (sonnet, weekly): updates OST, dedupes. opportunity-scorer (sonnet, weekly): scores on reach x frequency x severity x addressability. assumption-designer (sonnet, per opportunity): designs falsifiable test plan. trio-deliberation-agent (opus, bi-weekly): build | test | park | kill with rationale. roadmap-synth (opus, monthly): kill list + doubled-down list. outcome-tracker (haiku, daily): outcome trend + flag if Outcome unchanged > 4 weeks. spec-linker (haiku, on PR open): verify opp_id in spec front-matter.

rule.statement: Route via intake-classifier (haiku) before escalating to opus. Only trio deliberation warrants opus; classification is deterministic and cheap.
rule.rationale: Running opus on every intake item is 10x the cost with no quality improvement for classification. The model tier should match the reasoning complexity of the task.

example.code: <role>You are the {agent}. Continuous Discovery (Torres) -- product-planning lens.</role> <inputs> <ost>{path to opportunity-solution-tree.md}</ost> <intake>{path to intake-queue.md}</intake> <outcomes>{path to outcomes-register.md}</outcomes> <delivered>{path to recent merged PRs with opp_id refs}</delivered> <segment_filter>{optional: limit to a user segment}</segment_filter> </inputs> <rules> - Classify every node as: outcome | opportunity | solution | assumption_test. - Reject inputs phrased as features; re-prompt for the underlying need. - Never declare "validated"; use "tested" with explicit pass/fail criteria. - Score opportunities with (reach * frequency * severity * addressability), 1-5 each. - Cap visible opportunities per Outcome at 7; archive overflow to parked/. - Output JSON matching {schema_path}; emit a markdown digest <= 60 lines. </rules> <task>{cadence-specific instruction}</task>

antipattern.description: Intake-classifier passing "add filter X" through without explicit few-shot rejection examples.
antipattern.reason: Provide at least 10 few-shot rejections of solution-phrased requests in the classifier prompt. Without examples, the classifier will accept solution framing at the same rate as humans do without training.

antipattern.description: OST-curator aggressively deduping and merging opportunities that cross different Outcomes.
antipattern.reason: Require human review on any merge that crosses Outcome boundaries. Cross-Outcome merges lose the linkage between an opportunity and its parent Outcome metric.

antipattern.description: Opportunity-scorer hallucinating reach numbers when analytics fetch fails.
antipattern.reason: Require a fetched_at timestamp and source URL in every score row. Reject scores without provenance rather than proceeding with fabricated numbers.

antipattern.description: Trio-deliberation agent rubber-stamping the highest-scoring opportunity every run.
antipattern.reason: Force the agent to nominate a devil's-advocate alternative on every run. Without this, the deliberation reduces to a score-sorter rather than a reasoning process.

antipattern.description: Outcome-tracker declaring improvement on noisy daily metrics after a single positive day.
antipattern.reason: Require a 2-week rolling window minimum before any Outcome status change. Single-day signals are noise at standard metric variance levels.

text.reference: OST node schema with evidence, score, status, and linked_specs fields
GitHub Actions workflow enforcing opp_id in SDD specs
